title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm bowl of traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, known as Mercimek Corbasi, a staple in Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and comforting flavors. This traditional recipe combines simple ingredients to create a hearty and nourishing dish, perfect for any season. It's often enjoyed with a squeeze of fresh lemon and a drizzle of chili-infused butter.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic cloves. Have your tomato paste and spices ready.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot and stir to combine with the onion mixture.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the hot water or broth. Add salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth. If you dont have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili-Mint Butter
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to create a roux. Stir in the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber) and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant. Immediately remove from heat.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Pour the hot chili-mint butter mixture over the blended lentil soup. Stir gently to incorporate some of the butter, reserving some for drizzling over individual servings. Serve the Mercimek Corbasi hot, garnished with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice from the wedges.

tips: For an extra creamy Mercimek Corbasi, ensure you blend the soup thoroughly until absolutely no lentil chunks remain. A powerful immersion blender works wonders here.

Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) in the butter sauce to your preference. For a spicier kick, add a little more; for milder, reduce the amount. The dried mint is crucial for authentic flavor.
Mercimek Corbasi is best served piping hot with a generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice. A side of crusty bread and a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or cilantro can elevate the experience.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Turkish cuisine with a bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This simple yet profoundly flavorful soup holds a special place in the hearts and on the tables of Turkish families, served year-round as a comforting starter, a light lunch, or a nourishing antidote to a chilly day. Packed with wholesome ingredients and boasting a beautifully smooth texture, this staple dish is not only incredibly delicious but also remarkably easy to prepare. If you're looking to explore the rich tapestry of Turkish soups and stews, there’s no better place to start than with this iconic red lentil creation.

## What is Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-BAH-suh," is a classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup renowned for its vibrant color, creamy texture, and deeply savory flavor profile. Unlike some lentil soups that are chunky and rustic, Mercimek Corbasi is typically pureed to a silky smoothness, making it incredibly comforting and easy to digest. At its core, the soup is made from split red lentils, simmered with aromatic vegetables like onions and garlic, often enriched with a touch of tomato paste, and seasoned with staple Turkish spices such as cumin and dried mint.

What truly sets this soup apart, however, is its distinctive finishing touch: a drizzle of "nane yagi" (mint butter) or "pul biberli tereyağı" (red pepper flake butter). This fragrant oil, infused with dried mint and often a sprinkle of vibrant red pepper flakes, is swirled into each serving, adding a burst of color, aroma, and a subtle zing that elevates the entire dish. Mercimek Corbasi is a testament to the simplicity and elegance of Turkish home cooking, offering a deeply satisfying experience that is both nutritious and incredibly flavourful. It’s a naturally gluten-free and easily adaptable vegetarian/vegan option, making it a versatile choice for any diet.

## Ingredients for Mercimek Corbasi

Creating an authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup requires a few key ingredients, most of which are readily available. Here's what you'll need:

*   **Red Lentils:** 1 cup (about 200g) split red lentils. These cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's smooth texture.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** 2 tablespoons, for sautéing the aromatics.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced.
*   **Tomato Paste (Domates Salçası):** 1 tablespoon. This adds depth of flavor and a lovely reddish hue.
*   **Spices:**
    *   1 teaspoon ground cumin (kimyon)
    *   1/2 teaspoon sweet paprika (tatlı toz biber)
    *   1/2 teaspoon dried mint (kuru nane) – for the soup base.
*   **Water or Broth:** 6-7 cups (1.5-1.75 liters) vegetable or chicken broth, or plain water. Broth will add more flavor.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **For the Nane Yagi (Mint Butter Topping):**
    *   2 tablespoons olive oil or butter
    *   1 teaspoon dried mint (kuru nane)
    *   1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes (pul biber), optional, for a little heat and color.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving – absolutely essential for squeezing over the finished soup!

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Follow these simple steps to create a delicious and authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup in your own kitchen:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Place the red lentils in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and helps prevent the soup from becoming cloudy. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil (or butter) over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, being careful not to brown them. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in the tomato paste, cumin, paprika, and 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen the flavor of the tomato paste. This step is crucial for developing the soup's robust taste.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in 6 cups of water or broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend the Soup:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.
6.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add the remaining 1 cup of water or broth, a little at a time, until you reach your desired consistency. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Bring the soup back to a gentle simmer for a few minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
7.  **Prepare the Nane Yagi (Mint Butter):** While the soup is simmering, prepare the topping. In a small saucepan or skillet, melt the olive oil or butter over medium-low heat. Add the remaining 1 teaspoon of dried mint and the red pepper flakes (if using). Cook for about 30 seconds, just until the mint becomes fragrant and the pepper flakes sizzle, being careful not to burn them. Remove from heat immediately.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Turkish Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the fragrant nane yagi over each serving. Garnish with a fresh lemon wedge on the side, allowing each diner to squeeze fresh lemon juice into their soup to brighten the flavors.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving that perfect bowl of Turkish Red Lentil Soup is easier with a few insider tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This is a small but mighty step. Rinsing removes starches and impurities, leading to a clearer, cleaner-tasting soup.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Tomato Paste:** Cooking the tomato paste for a minute or two before adding liquid is key. This process, called "blooming," deepens its flavor and removes any raw taste.
*   **Season as You Go:** Taste your soup before and after blending. Lentils absorb a lot of salt, so you might need to adjust the seasoning at the end.
*   **The Power of the Immersion Blender:** An immersion blender is your best friend for Mercimek Corbasi. It makes achieving a silky-smooth texture effortless and mess-free.
*   **The Nane Yagi is Non-Negotiable:** While the soup is delicious on its own, the mint-infused butter or oil truly elevates it to an authentic Turkish experience. Don't skip it!
*   **Lemon is Essential:** The bright acidity of fresh lemon juice cuts through the richness of the lentils and spices, balancing the flavors perfectly. Always serve with a generous wedge.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is a versatile soup that can be enjoyed in many ways. Here are some traditional and complementary serving suggestions:

*   **With Fresh Lemon:** As mentioned, a squeeze of fresh lemon juice is non-negotiable. It brightens the soup and adds a crucial tangy note.
*   **Crusty Bread:** Serve alongside warm, crusty bread like pide, pita, or even a simple baguette, perfect for dipping and soaking up every last drop.
*   **A Simple Salad:** A light, fresh salad such as a shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) with tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions dressed with olive oil and lemon, offers a refreshing contrast.
*   **Olives and Pickles:** A side of briny olives or pickled vegetables (turşu) can provide a delightful textural and flavor counterpoint.
*   **Yogurt (Optional):** While not always traditional for this particular soup, a dollop of plain yogurt can be stirred in by those who enjoy a creamy, tangy addition.
*   **As a Starter:** Mercimek Corbasi makes an excellent appetizer before a larger Turkish meal, preparing the palate for more rich flavors.

## Variations and Substitutions for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

While the traditional recipe for Turkish Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, there are a few ways you can adapt it to suit your preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Make it Vegan:** The standard recipe is easily made vegan by simply using vegetable broth instead of chicken broth and olive oil instead of butter for both cooking and the Nane Yagi topping.
*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrients and body, you can add a small chopped carrot or potato along with the lentils. They will cook down and blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   **Spice it Up:** If you prefer a spicier soup, increase the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) in the Nane Yagi, or add a pinch of cayenne pepper to the soup base along with the other spices.
*   **Herbal Additions:** While dried mint is the star, a sprinkle of fresh parsley or cilantro as a garnish can add another layer of freshness.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth, though it deviates slightly from the traditional profile.
*   **Different Broths:** Experiment with different broths. While vegetable or chicken are standard, a light beef broth could add a richer, more umami flavor.

Mercimek Corbasi truly is a foundational dish in Turkish cuisine, celebrated for its comforting warmth, nourishing qualities, and incredibly satisfying flavor. Whether you’re a seasoned cook or just starting your culinary journey, this Turkish Red Lentil Soup offers a rewarding experience that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of Turkey. Give it a try – you won't be disappointed!
